caption: The pitch that NAND is now investable: ~$100B TAM ahead, slower/more efficient node migrations, and structurally lower industry capital investment.
NAND bit demand history and forecast: +35% CAGR historically, +13% recent, high-teens forecast to 2028; GenAI/LLM-training demand growing 29%/yr (TechInsights Q4 2024).
Industry NAND WFE capex and capital intensity CY2012-CY2026E: intensity peaked above 50% (2019-2022) and is cut to 26-28% in 2024E-2026E — the supply-discipline argument in one chart.
Cost curve slowdown: four 2D NAND generations cut cost/bit 24%/yr vs 11%/yr for four 3D generations — management's case that scaling economics now favor price/margin over deflation.
17 generations of NAND and >1,000x cost reduction (2001-2023), with world-first MLC, TLC and QLC — the technology lineage Sandisk claims from the legacy SanDisk era.
Scaling-strategy map: Sandisk reaches the same GB/cm2 with materially fewer layers than competition by leading in lateral/logical scaling — fewer layers means less deposition/etch capex per bit.
Capital-efficiency benchmark: industry flash capex per % petabyte output increase ran 1.44x-2.41x the Sandisk+Kioxia level (2018-2024, TrendForce) — the quantified moat claim for JV cost leadership.
BiCS8 QLC vs competition: +67% IO speed, 34% lower read latency, 30% better write power efficiency vs competitor Gen8 (11%/27%/28% vs Gen9) — Sandisk estimates.

Product segment strategy over the 1+ZB 2025 demand pool: aggressive growth in Data Center (298EB), sustained leadership in Client (277EB) and Consumer (59EB), deliberately selective in Mobile (372EB).
Client SSD portfolio: claims 'leading supplier' with 25%+ global client market share; note the still-WD-branded Blue/Green/Black lines sold under transitional license.
Client SSD technology transitions: QLC and PCIe Gen5 each ramp from near-zero in 2025 toward majority mix by 2028 — the roadmap behind client ASP/cost assumptions.
Enterprise SSD capacity roadmap: 128TB (CY25) to 256TB (CY26) to 512TB to 1PB (CY27) on the UltraQLC platform.
Consumer franchise economics: ~73% aided brand awareness and ~45% global revenue share in branded flash (2024 internal study) — the brand premium argument for the retail business.
Flash Ventures at scale: Sandisk+Kioxia produce nearly 1/3 of all NAND bits (LTM CQ3'24); megasite capacity is 80% JV / 20% Kioxia-only, JV output split 50/50.

The off-balance-sheet capex table: Sandisk's share of JV gross capex was $1,571M (16% of revenue) FY22, $1,407M (23%) FY23, $533M (8%) FY24, largely funded by subsidies/leases and tool depreciation charged through COGS.
Carve-out financial history: revenue $9,754M (FY22) to $6,086M (FY23) to $6,663M (FY24); GAAP gross margin 33% / 7% / 16% / 36% (1H'FY25) — the cyclicality Sandisk inherited.
Capitalization at separation: $1,339M cash vs ~$1,943M spin debt ($604M net debt); priority one is reaching net cash within four quarters, then balancing reinvestment, debt paydown and returns.
The through-cycle model: bit growth with market (mid-to-high teens), low-teens cost decline/yr, ~35% non-GAAP gross margin, ~20% operating margin, mid-teens gross capex, low-teens FCF margin.
Earnings power target: at $10B revenue Sandisk expects ~20% non-GAAP operating margin, $1.2B+ FCF, gross debt under $1B and net cash.
BiCS8 transition plan: 10% of customer bits by end-FY25, 40-50% by end-FY26, prominent node by late FY26/early CY27 — plus CY25 gross capex guided mid-to-high teens % of revenue.
The memory-wall setup for HBF: DRAM cost/GB now scaling <5%/yr and HBM rising from 48% to 68% of GPU-card BOM cost (2020-2025) — the opening Sandisk sees for flash in AI memory.
Sandisk 3D Matrix Memory: DRAM-like performance at 4x capacity and 50% bit cost, CXL-compatible, with a cost advantage vs DRAM projected to widen over six years.

High Bandwidth Flash (HBF): 16-die stack matching HBM bandwidth with 8-16x capacity at similar cost, enabled by BiCS+CBA and proprietary low-warpage stacking; architecture developed with major AI players.
HBF generational roadmap: Gen2/Gen3 target 1.5x/2x capacity, 1.45x/2x read bandwidth and 0.8x/0.64x energy per bit vs Gen1.

FY2025 true capital intensity: Sandisk share of JV gross capex $686M + PP&E $204M = $890M gross capex (12.1% of revenue), while cash capex was net negative — the recurring reconciliation of JV-funded tooling.

Flash Ventures operational framework: wafers purchased at cost plus small markup, Sandisk pays JV expenses (incl. equipment depreciation) and funds equipment via loans/equity/lease guarantees — the fixed-cost obligation in both directions.
Q1 FY2026 summary: revenue $2.3B, non-GAAP GM 29.9%, adjusted FCF $448M; demand outpacing supply and net cash reached ~6 months ahead of the Investor Day target.
Q2 FY2026 inflection: ASP/GB up mid-30s% QoQ on bits up only low-single digits; revenue ~$3.0B, non-GAAP GM 51.1%, EPS $6.20, adjusted FCF $843M.
Q3 FY2026 summary: revenue $5.95B, non-GAAP GM 78.4%, EPS $23.41, adjusted FCF $2,955M; three NBM partnerships secured (two more in Q4) and a $6B buyback after full debt repayment.
The mix shift in one chart: Datacenter $1,467M (+233% QoQ), Edge $3,663M (+118%), Consumer $820M (-10%, seasonal) — Consumer falls to ~14% of revenue vs 31% in FY2025.
